channel.title: Posts on Jonayed Hossan Gazi
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Jonayed Hossan Gazi

item.title: The simplest blog is just text files
item.description: Moved zuna.id from WordPress to Hugo on GitHub Pages. No server, no database, no maintenance.

item.title: Google’s NotebookLM: A Game-Changer for Education and a Prime Example of AI’s Potential
item.description: Google's NotebookLM is a source-grounded AI research assistant that's transforming how students, educators, and researchers interact with information.

item.title: 100GB free storage + server-side scripting for $0 — here is how
item.description: 100GB storage, server-side scripting, SSL, and CDN — all free. This is not a loss leader. Lua efficiency makes it structurally impossible for competitors to match.

item.title: A decade of code, zero forced migrations
item.description: Code written in 2013 still runs on Wapka today — unchanged alongside code from 2026. Here is how backward compatibility became a design principle, not an afterthought.

item.title: Between Wix and Vercel there is a void — here is what fills it
item.description: The web platform market has two extremes: walled gardens for beginners and DevOps for developers. Between them is a void. Wapka was built to fill it.

item.title: Building for the long tail, not the enterprise
item.description: Wapka does not target enterprises. It targets individuals, creators, students, and small teams. That is a strategic choice.

item.title: Built by people around the world: the Wapka community story
item.description: Wapka is not one person's creation. It is the sum of ideas, effort, and contributions from thousands of people across the world.

item.title: From Wapka user to Wapka developer: the career path
item.description: The upgrade path from visual builder to Lua scripting to REST API to self-hosting. One platform at every skill level. No migration needed. No starting over.

item.title: From zero coding knowledge to shipping a platform
item.description: I learned to code by building a web platform from scratch, without formal training. Here is what that process actually looks like across two years of iteration.

item.title: How I acquired what seemed impossible
item.description: From a $10 Sedo offer with no PayPal to owning zuna.id and banglad.sh — a decade of learning to acquire domains when every door seemed closed.

item.title: How Wapka grew from zero to 100,000 sites
item.description: A web platform ended its run. Rather than move on, I chose to rebuild it. Today over 100,000 sites run on the open-source platform that emerged from that decision.

item.title: How Wapka's request router handles three eras simultaneously
item.description: A single web request arrives at Wapka. It could match a legacy WAP tag page, a Lua route, or a Native App. Here is the architecture that dispatches across three eras.

item.title: Keeping terabytes of user data alive for a decade — and counting
item.description: Users upload media, files, and entire streaming sites. For over a decade, I kept every byte safe. Here is what it takes to maintain that promise at scale.

item.title: Pre-built modules: forums, galleries, user systems in one click
item.description: Forums, chatrooms, galleries, guestbooks, user systems, blogs, file managers — all built in. No plugin marketplace. No installation. No compatibility concerns.

item.title: Real-time chat and WebSockets on shared hosting
item.description: Real-time chat, notifications, and live updates — running on shared hosting without dedicated servers. How Wapka handles WebSockets at scale.

item.title: Self-host vs managed: the honest tradeoffs
item.description: Should you run Wapka on your own server or use the managed cloud? An honest comparison of what you gain and what you pay for with each choice.

item.title: SSL, CDN, social login — the infrastructure layer you don't have to think about
item.description: Free SSL certificates, global CDN, DDoS protection, social authentication. The platform handles DevOps so users don't have to.

item.title: The best platform for students learning to code is free and you've never heard of it
item.description: Most platforms charge before you can even start. Wapka gives you a full-stack environment for $0 — no credit card, no DevOps, no limits.

item.title: The Dataset: build a data layer without writing a single query
item.description: Wapka's built-in NoSQL database — zero configuration, schemaless, fully managed. Create collections, store documents, query with filters.

item.title: The detach/reattach protocol: how it works
item.description: Export your database. Run Docker. Your site is live on your server. Reattach anytime. Here is the technical walkthrough.

item.title: The doors open both ways — why Wapka is built for freedom
item.description: Open source on GitHub. Data export in JSON. Docker self-hosting for enterprise. The platform gives you every path to leave — and that freedom builds genuine trust.

item.title: The Express-like Lua framework: familiar syntax, sandboxed runtime
item.description: Wapka's Lua framework looks like Express.js — routes, middleware, request handling, database access. Familiar syntax running inside a secure PHP LuaSandbox environment.

item.title: The file manager that serves terabytes without breaking
item.description: Users upload everything from profile pictures to full media streaming libraries. The file manager handles it all — small files and terabytes of data on shared infrastructure.

item.title: The responsibility of hosting other people's work for over a decade
item.description: When someone builds their site on your platform, they trust you with their work. That trust is heavier than code. Here is what being a responsible steward means.

item.title: The theming engine: how users create and share designs
item.description: A community-driven design ecosystem. Users create Twig templates, customize layouts, and share designs. The platform visual identity emerges from the people who use it.

item.title: The visual builder that coexists with server-side code
item.description: Wix does visual but no backend. Vercel does code but no visual builder. Wapka does both — and they work together on the same platform, seamlessly.

item.title: Wapka vs WordPress vs Wix vs Vercel: an honest comparison
item.description: Wapka vs WordPress, Wix, and Vercel — an honest comparison across every dimension. Where Wapka wins, where it does not, and how to choose what fits your needs.

item.title: Wapka: the complete platform review
item.description: An open-source, AI-ready web platform combining a visual builder, Lua scripting, and backward-compatible legacy support. Reviewed across seven categories with an honest verdict.

item.title: What 10 years of building teaches you
item.description: A decade with one platform. Here is what I learned about consistency over intensity, backward compatibility as a principle, and why the long game always wins.

item.title: What is coming: the Wapka roadmap
item.description: Native apps. Advanced Lua framework. Full OpenAPI REST. Self-hosting improvements. Here is where the platform is heading.

item.title: Why I introduced paid plans after years of free
item.description: Free forever was the promise. But storage at scale costs real money. Here is how we introduced paid tiers without betraying the trust of the community.

item.title: Why I open-sourced Wapka
item.description: Open-sourcing years of work was not the obvious choice. But I believe trust is the only real moat — and you cannot build trust behind closed doors. Here is why.

item.title: Why Wapka runs on Lua — the architectural decision behind it
item.description: Choosing Lua for Wapka's scripting engine wasn't a compromise. It was the only correct engineering decision for a shared hosting platform at scale.

item.title: Why Wapka's compute costs are near zero
item.description: Why Wapka can offer server-side scripting for free. The numbers show how Lua via PHP LuaSandbox makes compute costs near zero on shared infrastructure.

item.title: Your AI agent can now build and manage websites — here is how Wapka does it
item.description: Wapka supports the Model Context Protocol. AI models can read your site data, modify content, and deploy features — without human intervention.

item.title: Manus AI: The Revolutionary Autonomous AI Agent
item.description: Discover Manus AI, the world's first fully autonomous AI agent that can handle complex tasks independently. Learn about its features, capabilities, and how it could change the future of work and life.

item.title: Python Guide: From Beginner to Pro
item.description: Your one-stop guide to mastering Python. This comprehensive blog post covers everything from the basics to advanced concepts, perfect for beginners and intermediate learners. Start your Python journey today!

item.title: Gladiator – the Epic Tale of Vengeance, Justice, and Resilience
item.description: The Roman Empire, with its grandeur, intrigue, and brutal spectacles, has always fascinated me, and few films capture its essence as powerfully as Gladiator (2000).

item.title: The Best Free Web Hosting Control Panel I’ve Used: Why I Chose Virtualmin Over cPanel
item.description: Discover why Virtualmin is the best free alternative to cPanel for managing web hosting. A personal comparison of control panels including CyberPanel, Webmin, and more.

item.title: The Past Is a Place of Reference, Not Residence
item.description: Life goes on, and so can you. A reflection on purpose, relationships, and why no one enters your life by accident.

item.title: When You Pass the Tree Twice, You’re Surely Lost!
item.description: When you pass the tree twice, you are surely lost — a metaphor for life choices, emotional loops, and the patterns we repeat.

item.title: Eyes to a Wider World: Reading Mother by Maxim Gorky
item.description: Reading Mother by Maxim Gorky introduced me to the struggles of Russian workers and the spirit of revolution. A quiet but powerful discovery that still shapes my appreciation of human resilience.

item.title: The Brighter the Flame, the Darker the Shadow It Casts: A Journey of Self-Realization
item.description: Explore the philosophical meaning of “The brighter the flame, the darker the shadow it casts” and how it relates to self-realization, ego, and shadow work.

item.title: “It’s Better to Be Slapped by the Truth Than Kissed by a Lie” – A Powerful Russian Proverb That Still Matters Today
item.description: Explore the meaning and timeless wisdom behind the Russian proverb “It’s better to be slapped by the truth than kissed by a lie.” Learn how honesty, even when painful, leads to growth.

item.title: How Text Editors and IDEs Made My Development Super Fast: From Notepad to VS Code, NetBeans, and PyCharm
item.description: See how moving from Notepad to powerful tools like VS Code, NetBeans, and PyCharm transformed my development speed and changed my coding journey forever.

item.title: Best Text Editors for Developers and Everyday Users: Notepad, Sublime Text, VS Code, Nano & Vim Explained
item.description: Explore the best text editors for both developers and general users. Learn how Notepad, Sublime Text, VS Code, Nano, and Vim boost productivity, coding, and everyday editing.

item.title: Easy Tunneling Explained: How to Run Your Local App Online with Ngrok, FRP, Cloudflare, and SSH Tunnels
item.description: Discover how to expose your local web apps to the internet using tunneling tools like Ngrok, FRP, Cloudflare Tunnel, and SSH. A beginner-friendly guide to understand tunneling and run apps globally.

item.title: How to Create a Bootable Linux or Windows USB Using Rufus and SD Memory Card Formatter
item.description: Learn step-by-step how to create a bootable USB for Linux or Windows using Rufus and SD Memory Card Formatter. Simple guide for beginners to install OS from USB drive.

item.title: Building a Bangla-Specific Search Engine: A Comprehensive Guide
item.description: Introduction This document provides a comprehensive guide to developing a modern, functional search engine specifically tailored for Bangladeshi origin and Bangla-language websites.

item.title: Android Development with Kotlin: A Beginner’s Guide
item.description: Welcome to the exciting world of Android app development with Kotlin!

item.title: Exploring the Wapka URL Library: A Comprehensive Guide to URL Processing
item.description: Discover how to use Wapka’s URL Library to encode, decode, parse, and rewrite URLs for mobile websites. Learn with examples and best practices for SEO-friendly URLs.

item.title: Understanding Wapka’s Request Library: A Complete Guide to Handling HTTP Requests
item.description: Learn how to use Wapka’s Request Library to handle HTTP requests in Lua. Explore the req table, its hierarchy, and practical examples for web development.

item.title: Exploring Wapka’s Server Library: A Comprehensive Guide to Handling HTTP Requests with Lua
item.description: Learn how to use Wapka’s server library in Lua to handle HTTP requests, manage templates, set headers, and more. A complete guide with examples for web developers.

item.title: Mastering Wapka’s Lua Sandbox Environment for Web Development
item.description: Explore Wapka’s Lua Sandbox Environment, a secure and customized Lua 5.1 platform for web development. Learn about its features, libraries, and how to get started.

item.title: Gods of Egypt – A Mythical Ride Through Divine Chaos
item.description: If you’re in the mood for a visually spectacular, action-packed fantasy that dives into the clash of gods and mortals, Gods of Egypt (2016) is a wild ride worth taking.

item.title: How to Install an HTML Template on Wapka and Configure a Reverse Proxy
item.description: Learn how to install an HTML template on Wapka and configure a reverse proxy with NGINX. Create ad-free, dynamic websites with Wapka’s powerful features.

item.title: How to Create a Wapka Project: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Learn how to create a Wapka project with this step-by-step guide. Discover Wapka’s free plan, 100 GB cloud storage, and easy-to-use control panel for web development.

item.title: Introduction to Wapka: The Ultimate Web Development Platform
item.description: Discover Wapka: Your Go-To Web Development Platform and CMS for Building Modern Web Applications Are you ready to take your web development journey to new heights?
